column2: Baseline QALE by IMD quintile, Love-Koh et al. (2023), PharmacoEconomics
Opportunity-cost distribution, miqdadasaria/DCEA repo, net_health_benefits_universal_distribution.csv, commit e8c482e (Nov 2019)
Thresholds = £20,000/QALY (NICE base case); £12,936/QALY (Claxton et al. 2015, empirical)
DCEA framework = Asaria, Griffin & Cookson (2016), Medical Decision Making
Comparator study = Malcolm et al., YHEC/Novo Nordisk (DCEA of semaglutide, HPR58)
Per-person vs total: baseline QALE and net-benefit-per-person are per-individual; opportunity cost, patients treated, and total net benefit are population totals. Convert only by × or ÷ population.
Opportunity cost: total displaced = net cost × patients treated ÷ threshold, distributed across quintiles by chosen share.
Welfare: Atkinson equally-distributed equivalent (EDE) on post-drug QALE across inequality-aversion levels.
